source: Pinned snapshot. Ground truth = published bug-bounty scope ∪ subfinder@2.14.0 + amass@v4 + crt.sh validated set as of pin date.
ethics_note: All organizations have an active bug-bounty program with a published in-scope list. Scoring is on the published scope only; assets discovered outside scope are recorded but never penalized.

subjects.notes: Reference target from Black Lantern 2022 face-off. Largest delta vs that study (373 → 1291).
Outlier — Cloudflare exposes a large footprint via cf-N-N-N-N hostnames. Useful for stress-testing recall on long-tail edge subdomains.
Surprisingly small public footprint despite being THE bug-bounty platform. Useful as a precision control — tools that hallucinate subdomains will inflate FP here.
Pending pin — populated on first run.
Pending pin — populated on first run.
Pending pin — populated on first run.

open_methodology_risks: Ground truth is currently subfinder's output, which is itself one of the subjects under test. v2 of the corpus will substitute Chaos's published asset feeds + crt.sh to break this circularity.
Hackerone.com's 24-domain ground-truth is small enough that DNS rotation could move the score noticeably. Re-pin on every quarterly refresh.
